text.title: Margin Defense — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules covering the 6-pattern bleed detection, cadence, and the client-conversation trigger.

rule.statement: The detector scans for SIX patterns weekly: (1) SILENT-SCOPE-CREEP (deliverable extended without change-request), (2) FREE-ANALYSIS (vendor billed < 0.5h for what was an analysis session ≥ 1h), (3) MISSING-CHANGE-REQUEST (work done outside SOW with no CR), (4) AI-REWORK-LOOP (same task billed ≥ 3 times in a sprint), (5) GOLD-PLATING (effort spent on un-requested polish), (6) SYMPATHY-DISCOUNT (vendor self-discounted to "be nice").

rationale.source: Patrick McKenzie consulting essays / Jonathan Stark / observed micro-agency P&L

rule.rationale: These 6 patterns account for ~80% of fixed-price margin erosion across documented agency cohorts. Other leaks (bad estimation, exchange-rate, tooling cost) belong in pricing methodologies, not bleed detection.

rule.statement: Detector runs WEEKLY (Friday end-of-week). Daily is noise; monthly accumulates uncatchable drift. Detector reads time-tracking + comms thread + SOW.
rule.rationale: Weekly aligns with billing/check-in rhythm. Friday catches the week's bleeds before they compound across the weekend or into the next sprint.

rule.statement: Any single pattern producing bleed > 5% of total project margin triggers an alert with a client-conversation suggested within 48 business hours. Cumulative bleed across patterns > 10% triggers a margin-reset conversation.

rationale.source: Project-margin cohort studies (PSA tooling vendors — FunctionFox, Mavenlink/Kantata)

rule.rationale: 5% per pattern is the threshold at which silent absorption becomes economically painful for solo / micro-agency. 10% cumulative is where the project moves from "still profitable" to "vendor working for free".

rule.statement: Every detected scope change MUST trigger a written change request (CR template) BEFORE additional work is done. Verbal acknowledgments don't count. If client refuses to sign a CR, work stops at the SOW boundary.

rationale.source: PMI PMBOK change-control discipline + small-agency operating norm

rule.rationale: Verbal "yes, do that" rots into "we never agreed to pay for that" within 30 days. Written CR is the only collateral the vendor has.

rule.statement: Discovery, analysis, and "quick calls" are BILLABLE by default. The vendor may choose to discount specific items, but the time-tracker logs them at full rate first and any discount is logged as `write_off: SALES_INVESTMENT`. Default-free leaks margin invisibly.

rationale.source: Brennan Dunn — DYFR / Patrick McKenzie kalzumeus

rule.rationale: "Free" work that isn't logged disappears from the cost model. Logging at full rate THEN discounting preserves visibility. After 3 free calls the vendor sees the pattern; after 30 they're working at half the contracted rate.

example.title: Detecting SILENT-SCOPE-CREEP
example.code:
SOW deliverable: "Migrate user-auth from session to JWT (1 service)."
Week 3 time log: - 6h working on user-auth migration - 4h on admin-auth migration ← NOT in SOW - 2h on API-key auth migration ← NOT in SOW
Comms thread (Slack): client: "while you're in there, can you also do admin?" vendor: "sure!" ← verbal yes, no CR
Detection: Pattern: SILENT-SCOPE-CREEP Extra hours: 6h At vendor rate $150/hr → $900 As % of project margin ($8k margin): 11.25%
Alert: > 5% per r3 → trigger client conversation (use templates/margin-alert-message.md)
example.note: r3 + r4: detect, quantify, alert, draft message.

example.title: FREE-ANALYSIS absorbed (r5 violation)
example.code:
Vendor agreed to "a quick 30-min call to review approach". Call ran 75 minutes. Time log: 0.5h (vendor rounded down because "they were nice"). Actual cost: 1.25h × $150 = $187.50 absorbed.
Repeated 4× per sprint → $750 in a single sprint → ~5% margin lost. Detector: log full 1.25h THEN discount with reason. Now visible.
example.note: Default-free leaks compound; r5 makes them visible.
